ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general pizza class (FOODON_00002426). No specific merguez or harissa pizza subclass exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers pizza as a category. No specific merguez-harissa variant exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code exists for merguez and harissa pizza. General pizza food concept not mapped in clinical terminology for this dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One whole 12-inch pizza, approximately 500 g, typically served as a single-person meal at a restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pizza with meat, NS as to type of crust (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite); supplemented with USDA entries for merguez-style lamb-beef sausage and harissa paste components
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for merguez and harissa pizza. Values estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for meat pizza (FDC ID 173961, 'Pizza, meat topping, regular crust') as the base, adjusted upward for fat and protein to reflect merguez sausage (higher fat lamb-beef blend) and harissa paste contribution. Rocket leaves and roasted capsicum contribute negligible macros at typical topping quantities. Per-100g values reflect a fully assembled baked pizza. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Pizza entry: Pizza, cheese, regular crust (GI ~50, referenced against glucose).
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for merguez and harissa pizza. The closest analogous food in the University of Sydney GI Database is regular-crust cheese and meat pizza (GI approximately 50, tested against glucose). Merguez sausage and harissa paste are high-fat, high-protein toppings that would not raise GI above the base pizza crust value; if anything, the higher fat and protein content would moderate postprandial glucose response. GI 50 is therefore a defensible upper-bound estimate. GL per serving calculated as (GI x carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (50 x 137.5) / 100 = 68.8, where carbohydrate per 500g serving = 27.5 x 5 = 137.5g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~620 mg per 100g; ~3100 mg per whole pizza)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this pizza due to its very high sodium load from merguez sausage, harissa paste, mozzarella, and tomato sauce; a single whole pizza can exceed the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving despite moderate GI, driven by large refined-carbohydrate portion from pizza dough
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with portion size; while the GI of regular-crust pizza is moderate (~50), consuming a whole 12-inch pizza delivers approximately 137g of carbohydrate, producing a very high glycemic load (GL ~69) that will significantly elevate postprandial blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from merguez sausage (lamb and beef) and buffalo mozzarella, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this pizza due to its combined burden of saturated fat (~4.9g per 100g, ~24.5g per whole pizza) and high sodium, both of which are established risk factors for adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Merguez e Harissa is a 12-inch Italian-style pizza served at Ricciotti restaurant in Singapore, distinguished by its North African-inspired toppings. The pizza features homemade merguez sausages made from a spiced lamb and beef blend, roasted capsicum (red bell pepper), harissa hot chilli pepper paste, classic tomato sauce, certified Buffalo Mozzarella D.O.P. (Denominazione di Origine Protetta), and fresh rocket (arugula) leaves. The base is a standard Italian pizza crust baked in a wood-fired or deck oven. Merguez is a North African fresh sausage seasoned with cumin, coriander, chilli, and garlic, giving the pizza a bold, spicy, and smoky flavour profile. Harissa paste adds further heat and a deep red colour. The rocket leaves provide a peppery, slightly bitter contrast to the richness of the cheese and sausage. Nutritionally, this pizza is energy-dense (~266 kcal per 100g) with moderate protein (~12.5g), moderate-to-high fat (~11.8g, of which ~4.9g is saturated), and a significant carbohydrate load from the dough (~27.5g per 100g). Sodium is notably high (~620 mg per 100g) due to the cured sausage, harissa, and cheese. Clinically, the dish poses concerns for individuals managing hypertension (very high sodium per serving), Type 2 Diabetes (high glycemic load from the large dough portion), and cardiovascular disease (saturated fat and sodium). The GI is estimated at approximately 50 (low-moderate, analogous to regular-crust meat pizza). This dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those avoiding gluten or dairy.
common_user_queries: What is merguez and harissa pizza?
What is in Ricciotti merguez pizza?
Is merguez pizza suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: No exact match for merguez and harissa pizza exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS). Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for regular-crust meat pizza as the closest analogous base, with adjustments for the higher-fat merguez sausage topping. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for regular-crust pizza. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy 'Pizza, meat topping, regular crust' (FDC ID 173961) as the closest analogous base, adjusted for merguez sausage fat and protein content. No exact approved-source entry exists for this specific dish. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pizza reference entry. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pizza reference entry. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy pizza reference entry. 5. GI is estimated at 50 based on the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for regular-crust cheese and meat pizza; no direct GI measurement exists for merguez and harissa pizza. gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule. 6. The input record incorrectly classifies this dish under cuisine 'Italian' with cuisine_tag 'Chicken Dish'; the dish contains no chicken. The cuisine tag has been corrected; the Italian cuisine classification is retained as the pizza style is Italian. 7. Halal status is not confirmed; merguez sausage is traditionally halal in North African cuisine but the restaurant's sourcing is not verified. 8. The serving size of 500g is an estimate for a whole 12-inch pizza; actual weight may vary by restaurant. 9. Glycemic load per serving = (GI 50 × carbohydrate per serving 137.5g) / 100 = 68.8. 10. This is a Singapore restaurant dish (field survey data, Ricciotti - The Riverwalk); multilingual names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are not applicable as this is an Italian-cuisine dish, not a Singaporean dish. The multilingual fields are left null accordingly.
